oneSentencePitch: A centralized directory for Medicare beneficiaries to easily find licensed providers and their appeal decisions.
targetUser: Medicare beneficiaries seeking licensed healthcare providers.

deliverables: Shortlist feature for users to save providers
Landing page with a clear CTA
mvpDefinition.successMetric: At least 10 target users perform a search in the first 14 days

steps: Identify and obtain the official Medicare provider dataset from CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services).
Normalize the dataset into a minimal searchable schema (e.g., provider name, specialty, location, appeal decisions).
Build a search interface allowing users to filter by specialty and location.
Create detail pages for each provider displaying key information and appeal decisions.
Implement a shortlist feature allowing users to save providers for later reference.
Set up tracking for searches, detail views, and outbound clicks.
Publish a landing page with a clear call-to-action to start searching.
risks: Dataset may not be comprehensive or up-to-date, affecting user trust.
Low initial user engagement may hinder validation.
Technical issues with search functionality could frustrate users.

executionContext.trigger: Users need to find licensed Medicare providers quickly and easily.
coreInputs: Official Medicare provider dataset from CMS.
coreOutputs: A searchable directory of Medicare providers with detailed information.
mustHaveV1Features: Search functionality by specialty and location
suggestedIntegrations: CMS API for ongoing dataset updates (if available)
Google Analytics for tracking user interactions
operationalNotes: Keep the dataset updated by scheduling regular imports from CMS.
Monitor user engagement metrics to iterate on features.

goNoGo: At least 10 target users perform a search in the first 14 days
At least 3 users click through to a provider or save a shortlist
